channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Conflict in Lebanon reaches previously unaffected cities, including Sidon and Beirut suburbs
item.description: Fighting in Lebanon has escalated beyond its traditional Hezbollah strongholds, marking a significant expansion of the ongoing regional conflict. On Tuesday, Israeli air strikes hit Sidon, a mainly Su...

item.title: Parents Urged to Take Children with Ear Problems to Health Facilities
item.description: In Mtwara, parents and the general community have been urged to avoid using traditional remedies to treat ear problems in children and instead seek professional medical care. The call came from Colone...

item.title: Sundowns edge past Arrows to stay level with Pirates in PSL title race
item.description: In an exciting night of Betway Premiership action at Loftus Versfeld, Mamelodi Sundowns secured a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Lamontville Golden Arrows despite playing with 10 men for much of the sec...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney Announces Major Senior Public Service Reshuffle Focused on Leadership Renewal
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has announced a significant shuffle of senior positions across Canada’s federal public service, continuing his effort to shape the government’s leadership team. The restruct...

item.title: Fauji Fertiliser gets green light to join consortium acquiring 75% of PIA
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Privatisation Commission has formally approved Fauji Fertiliser Company (FFC) to become part of the consortium acquiring a 75% stake in Pakistan International Airlines (PIA). This appro...

item.title: Explaining the Philippines’ Crisis Alert Levels for Filipinos Abroad
item.description: The Philippines has a structured crisis alert system designed to guide overseas Filipinos during emergencies, conflicts, or health hazards. Managed by the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and the D...

item.title: Key Lessons from FAAN’s Transition to Cashless Public Payments
item.description: The Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) don waka strong cashless payment system to reduce money leakages and improve accountability for public funds. As dem dey roll am out for Lagos and Abuj...

item.title: NDIS staff warn algorithm-driven planning overhaul is off track
item.description: Senior staff at the National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA) have raised serious concerns over the rollout of the so-called New Framework Planning (NFP), which aims to replace human involvement in ...

item.title: Boko Haram Attacks Military Base and IDP Camp in Ngoshe, Borno; Many Killed
item.description: Early on Wednesday, 4th March 2026, Ngoshe town for Gwoza Local Government Area, Borno State, waka enter wahala as Boko Haram terrorists of the Islamic State West Africa Province (ISWAP) carry out hea...

item.title: John Crace criticises Kemi Badenoch's aggressive stance at PMQs amid US-Iran conflict
item.description: In his latest politics sketch, John Crace lambasts Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch for what he describes as a deeply flawed and borderline disgraceful performance during Prime Minister's Questions o...

item.title: Kenya Expands Irrigation Projects with World Bank and UNOPS to Improve Food Security
item.description: Kenya is pushing forward its irrigation agenda through strengthened collaborations with the World Bank and UNOPS. On March 3, Principal Secretary for Irrigation, Ephantus Kimotho, met with the new Wat...

item.title: Two Suspected Militants Killed in Joint CTD and Police Operation in Sarai Naurang
item.description: In a joint operation conducted by the Counter Terrorism Department (CTD) and local police in Sarai Naurang, Bannu and Lakki Marwat officials successfully killed two suspected militants late on Wednesd...

item.title: Orlando Pirates edge Polokwane City 2-1 to stay in title hunt
item.description: Orlando Pirates maintained their push for the Betway Premiership title by defeating Polokwane City 2-1 at Old Peter Mokaba Stadium on Wednesday night, staying level on 44 points with Mamelodi Sundowns...

item.title: FIFA Confirms DR Congo in 2026 World Cup Playoffs, Nigeria’s Qualification Hopes End
item.description: Nigeria fans don dey feel heavy wahala as FIFA don officially confirm Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo) among the six teams wey go play for the 2026 World Cup intercontinental playoff. This one ...

item.title: Dar calls for SCO Development Bank and stronger regional connectivity at Moscow summit
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Senator Ishaq Dar represented Pakistan at the 24th Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Council of Heads of Government meeting held in Moscow from Novembe...

item.title: DFA says China deal helped safely support Filipino troops at Ayungin Shoal
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) defended the Philippines’ July 2024 arrangement with China, emphasizing that it has not affected national sovereignty or maritime rights. According to DFA marit...

item.title: Tanzania Sees Significant Fuel Price Increase in March 2026 Due to Gulf Market
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Fuel prices in Tanzania have gone up sharply in March 2026 following rising costs in the Arabian Gulf market. According to the Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA), ...

item.title: PM Nchemba Listens to 305 Complaints from Mbulu Residents During Public Rally
item.description: During a public rally at Mbulu Town football ground, Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba received a total of 305 complaints and proposals from wananchi of Mbulu District in Manyara Region. The PM emphasize...

item.title: White House Outlines Iran Military Objectives Without Addressing Support for Iranian People
item.description: In the wake of recent US military action against Iran, the White House has outlined its objectives, but made no mention of supporting the Iranian people or promoting regime change. Press Secretary Kar...

item.title: Why India Prefers Russian and French Fighter Jets Over American Ones
item.description: India has consistently avoided purchasing American fighter jets despite multiple offers over the past two decades. The historical reasons trace back to the Cold War, when the United States supplied Pa...
